methodsAttempted.query: site:uark.edu ("ChatGPT" OR "generative AI" OR "artificial intelligence") (policy OR guidelines OR academic integrity OR teaching OR privacy) "University of Arkansas"
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the University Libraries AI and Academic Integrity guide, Academic Initiatives and Integrity faculty resources, Arkansas News articles about the central AI site and approved tools, and the central ai.uark.edu site.

methodsAttempted.query: site:uark.edu "acceptable use of artificial intelligence" "University of Arkansas"
methodsAttempted.notes: Search confirmed the official Arkansas News article linking to the Guidelines for Acceptable Use of Artificial Intelligence and an official Walton College page that summarizes the campus AI site.

The central AI site pages were discoverable and extractable, but https://ai.uark.edu/robots.txt returns User-agent: * Disallow: /, so they were recorded as robots-disallowed candidates and not used as evidence snapshots.
Robots.txt disallows /search and /articles/archive but permits the specific public news article used for the approved-tools and acceptable-use-guidelines evidence.
Robots.txt for general user agents disallows selected dynamic endpoints but permits the public AI and Academic Integrity guide page used for student-facing academic-integrity evidence.
The public Academic Initiatives and Integrity faculty resources page was fetched successfully and used for syllabus-guidance evidence.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a central University of Arkansas AI site, but that host disallows all crawling in robots.txt. Evidence therefore uses crawl-allowed official UARK pages: an Arkansas News campus advisory about approved tools and the acceptable-use guidelines, the University Libraries AI and Academic Integrity guide, and the Academic Initiatives and Integrity faculty resources page. Claims are limited to what those accessible sources directly say.

artifacts.title: University of Arkansas Fayetteville AI Policy Evidence Review
artifacts.summary: Staged candidate evidence for University of Arkansas Fayetteville uses crawl-allowed official UARK sources. The central ai.uark.edu guidelines and tools pages were discovered but excluded from evidence because ai.uark.edu/robots.txt disallows crawling for all user agents. Approved claims cover the January 2026 official advisory on acceptable-use guidelines, approved AI tools, unapproved-tool exceptions, University Libraries guidance requiring instructor permission for generative AI in academic work, citation expectations for AI-generated text, and Academic Initiatives and Integrity syllabus guidance for faculty.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Central ai.uark.edu guidelines and tools pages were not used as evidence because robots.txt disallows crawling for all user agents.
Approved-tools evidence is time-scoped to the January 20, 2026 official advisory and should be refreshed against crawl-allowed current official pages when available.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
